title: erica tetralix subsp. mackayi
intro: Erica tetralix subsp. mackayi, also known as heath, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving stress and anxiety, and promoting a healthy immune system. Erica tetralix subsp. mackayi can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
